items.title: Govt invites bids to set up 10 GWh grid-scale advanced chemistry cell manufacturing
items.summary: In a bold move, the Ministry of Heavy Industries is inviting bids for extensive Advanced Chemistry Cell manufacturing facilities, with enticing incentives for achieving a total capacity of ten gigawatt hours as part of the PLI ACC Scheme. This initiative is focused on grid-scale stationary storage applications, aiming to strengthen local manufacturing and decrease import dependency. Impressively, forty gigawatt hours have already been allocated to domestic producers.

items.summary: The investor has been sharply critical of the stock market, one that he says is increasingly defined by speculative trading as opposed to long-term investing.

items.summary: Like at peers Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase, a massive beat in equities trading drove the quarter's outsized results.

items.title: Stripe, Advent make $53 billion takeover offer for PayPal, sending stock soaring
items.summary: Stripe and Advent International have made a joint offer to acquire PayPal for $60.50 per share, in a deal that would valued at more than $53 billion.

items.title: US stocks today: US stocks open higher as investors weigh earnings
items.summary: Wall Street opened higher as softer producer inflation data eased concerns over interest rates, while investors tracked a wave of corporate earnings. PayPal shares surged on a reported $53 billion takeover bid, lifting sentiment. Gains across major indexes reflected cautious optimism amid improving inflation signals and steady market momentum overall.

items.summary: Elara Securities

items.title: Euro zone bond yields tick higher along with oil prices after big swings
items.summary: Bond yields in the Euro zone experienced a slight uptick on Wednesday, driven by rising oil prices linked to the U.S.-Iran tensions that threaten energy channels. Germany's 2-year bond yield reacted accordingly, highlighting concerns over future central bank rate increases. Although U.S. inflation reports suggested a decrease, the looming energy price pressures cast uncertainty over economic stability.

items.title: Groww profit nearly doubles in Q1, takes lead in retail commodity derivatives
items.summary: Groww profit after tax in Q1FY27 rises 94.4% year-on-year to ₹735 crore, driven by revenue growth

items.title: Union Bank Q1 Results: Profit rises over 27% to Rs 5,641 crore
items.summary: Union Bank of India achieved a remarkable 27.5% surge in its net profit for the June quarter. The bank experienced a subtle rise in net interest income, alongside a slight enhancement in margins. Additionally, the bank attracted deposits from overseas locations like Australia and the UAE through a specialized RBI window. Both global and domestic deposits increased, with a significant growth in low-cost CASA deposits as well.

items.title: Shipping ministry gives in-principle approvals for shipbuilding and repair clusters in Gujarat
items.summary: Gujarat will soon establish a new shipbuilding cluster in Porbandar. A significant ship repair facility is also planned for Vadinar in the Gulf of Kutch. These projects aim to boost India's maritime infrastructure and capacity. The Vadinar facility will include a long jetty and two large floating dry docks. Both initiatives are part of the Shipbuilding Development Scheme.

items.title: Japan PM Takaichi says draft economic blueprint not cause of bond market rout
items.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi believes that there's no connection between her administration's economic plan and the ongoing fluctuations in the market. She pointed out that interest rates and exchange rates are shaped by multiple global dynamics. Recent discussions about political influence on monetary policy have surged, particularly after the economic blueprint highlighted the crucial role of monetary policy for strengthening the economy.

items.title: Wholesale prices unexpectedly declined 0.3% in June on big drop in gasoline
items.summary: As with consumer prices, the index benefited from easing energy costs, particularly as oil fell due to the brief pause in tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

items.title: PFC raises USD 300 mn via bonds carrying floating interest rates
items.summary: Power Finance Corporation has successfully secured USD 300 million via floating rate notes set to mature in July 2029. These instruments adhere to the Reserve Bank of India's guidelines for external borrowing. The funds raised will be allocated effectively in accordance with these regulations, with the bonds slated for listing on NSE IFSC and India INX, thereby enhancing PFC's international market engagement and funding capabilities.

items.title: Warren Buffett tells CNBC he initiated Berkshire Hathaway's investment in Alphabet
items.summary: Warren Buffett said he was the driving force behind the conglomerate's recent big investment in Alphabet.

items.title: Amara Raja to phase lithium-ion cell rollout with focus on standard battery cells
items.summary: Amara Raja Energy & Mobility is entering lithium-ion cell manufacturing with a measured approach. The company will initially produce standard cells for electric two-wheelers and energy storage systems. Its Customer Qualification Plant is now operational for customer testing and validation. Commercial production at the first gigafactory is slated to begin next year. Passenger vehicle programs are part of long-term plans after demonstrating manufacturing capabilities.

items.title: Tata Elxsi shares fall 5% as margin miss weighs on sentiment
items.summary: Brokerages highlighted a sharp margin contraction and uncertainty in key business segments

items.title: Warburg Pincus buys Mumbai’s pharma company Integrace in ₹1,200 crore deal
items.summary: In a landmark transaction, Warburg Pincus, a prominent US private equity firm, has purchased Integrace Private Limited for ₹1,200 crore. With this deal, True North and Temasek Holdings have decided to divest entirely from their stakes in the company. Rehan Khan, a seasoned leader in the industry, will take the helm as CEO, steering Integrace's focus on its celebrated orthopedic and gynecological brands.

items.title: HDFC Asset Management Q1 net up 12% at ₹837 crore on better inflows, rise in AUM
items.summary: The quarterly average AUM of the fund house was up 13% at ₹9.35 lakh crore, while the market share was down marginally to 11.2% (11.5%)

items.title: Oil traders warn market is close to running on empty as Hormuz shuts again
items.summary: Stockpiles that acted as shock absorbers early in Iran war are running low as key waterway closes once more

items.title: Upmarket invasion: How India's quick commerce giants are crowding out specialist gourmet players
items.summary: Indian quick-commerce platforms are now targeting affluent households with premium products. This strategic shift aims to improve profitability by focusing on higher-margin items. Companies are introducing imported goods and artisanal produce to attract wealthier customers. While speed was once the key differentiator, range and quality now drive competition.

items.title: BlackRock assets hit record $15 trillion on boost from buoyant markets, ETF inflows
items.summary: In a remarkable show of strength, BlackRock announced impressive second-quarter profits that outpaced Wall Street's forecasts. Driven by a buoyant stock market, client assets surged to an unprecedented $15.34 trillion. The firm's iShares ETF franchise was a significant contributor, pulling in $192 billion in client cash. Additionally, private markets experienced a substantial influx of $15.4 billion, with private credit playing a crucial role.

items.title: First commercial consignments valued at $4,46,046 flagged off under CETA from Chennai
items.summary: India and the United Kingdom's CETA agreement officially began on Wednesday. First commercial shipments valued at over USD 446,000 were flagged off from Chennai. These inaugural consignments represented diverse sectors including jewellery and automotive components. Agricultural and processed food products were also part of the significant export shipment.

items.title: NY Fed's John Williams: "Unquestionably too-high" inflation may soon subside
items.summary: John Williams, the president of the New York Fed, asserts that inflation is likely at its peak and should soon begin to taper off. He projects that by the end of the year, overall inflation will settle at 3.25%. Furthermore, he expects the unemployment rate to drop to 4% by 2028, attributing this optimism to recent declines in energy prices. The Federal Reserve's latest meeting saw maintained policy rates.

items.title: Treasury yields are flat as traders weigh encouraging inflation data vs. oil rebound
items.summary: Treasury yields were edged higher as investors awaited the latest producer price inflation print for June.

items.title: Cabinet approves Rs 24,000 crore highways in Varanasi
items.summary: The Union Cabinet approved highway projects exceeding twenty-four thousand crore rupees for Varanasi. Railway multi-tracking projects in Odisha were also approved for nearly four thousand crore rupees. These initiatives aim to significantly decongest the temple town's road network. Improved connectivity will benefit key religious and cultural landmarks in Varanasi. The projects will enhance freight traffic and support regional economic growth.

items.title: India bonds recover on softer US rate outlook; oil risk caps gains
items.summary: Indian government bonds experienced a positive turnaround on Wednesday, recovering from previous dips as soft inflation data from the US eased rate hike apprehensions. However, higher crude oil prices exerted upward pressure on US yields, which limited market gains. Steady buying from state banks and foreign entities bolstered bonds, though traders continue to navigate inflationary and fiscal uncertainties.

items.title: AI is changing hospitality careers: Top travel jobs and skills likely to survive in the age of Artificial Intelligence
items.summary: Artificial intelligence is transforming travel operations and job roles. Many travel careers will remain resilient due to their human interaction focus, however, employers now value critical thinking and emotional intelligence over technical skills alone. Industry-integrated learning bridges the gap between education and future employment needs.

items.title: Morgan Stanley beats profit estimates on dealmaking boost, strong trading
items.summary: Morgan Stanley has showcased an impressive performance for the second quarter, surpassing Wall Street's earnings expectations. The surge in mergers and acquisitions has notably enhanced the investment bank's advisory revenues, while record trading profits emerged amid ongoing market fluctuations. Additionally, advancements in wealth management have played a pivotal role in fueling the bank's overall financial triumph, signifying a dynamic era for the financial services sector.

items.title: Dimon-led JPMorgan poised to become world's first $1 trillion bank
items.summary: JPMorgan Chase is approaching a monumental valuation of $1 trillion, marking a pivotal achievement for the financial institution. Its remarkable earnings have driven share prices to new heights, bringing it closer to this significant benchmark. The bank's prowess in both dealmaking and lending continues to support growth. Investors commend CEO Jamie Dimon's extensive leadership as a key factor, raising hopes for sustained high performance in the future.

items.title: Aurrevia launches Category III AIF with $10 million anchor from Kothari Family Office
items.summary: The fund targets sophisticated investors and positions itself around capital preservation, long-term compounding, and process-driven portfolio construction within a governance-focused investment mandate

items.title: Cube Highways Trust sets ₹151-152 price band for ₹5,000-crore InvIT IPO
items.summary: The offer comprises an offer for sale (OFS) by existing unitholders as the privately listed infrastructure investment trust (InvIT) transitions to a publicly listed InvIT

items.title: Bio-input demand may rise, but skill gap threatens India's green farming push: Agriculture Commissioner
items.summary: Agriculture Commissioner said widespread adoption of bio-inputs by India's nearly 14 crore farmers could generate annual demand of 20–30 crore tonnes.

items.title: Midnight social media curfew and limits to infinite scrolling proposed for older UK teens
items.summary: The U.K. government has proposed new measures to protect older teens on social media, including a midnight curfew and a limit to infinite scrolling.

items.title: NCDEX to roll out Guar Korma futures from July 24
items.summary: NCDEX will launch Guar Korma futures on July 24, giving exporters, processors and feed manufacturers a SEBI-regulated platform to hedge price risks. The contract completes the exchange-traded guar derivatives ecosystem, improves price discovery and is expected to support India's export-driven guar industry amid volatile global feed markets.

items.title: RBI greenlights three-year term for Rajiv Kumar as HDFC Bank chair
items.summary: RBI has approved Rajiv Kumar's appointment as part-time chairman of HDFC Bank for three years, succeeding interim chairman Keki Mistry.

items.title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | S&P 500 | US Stock Market Today | Live: US stocks open higher as investors weigh earnings; PayPal jumps
TVS Venu Group firm Home Credit India to acquire Varthana Finance for Rs 967 crore
items.summary: Home Credit India Finance will acquire Varthana Finance for Rs 967 crore. This all-cash transaction is subject to regulatory approvals from the Reserve Bank of India. The acquisition will strengthen TVS Venu Group's financial services sector presence. Varthana Finance specializes in education lending and serves many institutions. This move expands TVS's participation in secured and longer-tenure lending.

items.title: Cabinet okays National Investment Policy to add fresh 10 million tonnes urea capacity
items.summary: The government approved a new urea investment policy to establish new gas-based plants. This policy aims to create additional urea production capacity of ten million tonnes. It seeks to make India completely self-reliant in the widely consumed fertilizer. The new framework revises the 2012 policy with updated investment incentives. These measures will encourage new investments in the domestic urea manufacturing sector.

items.title: In-principle nod to 2 maritime projects, including Rs 1,570 cr ship repair facility in Gujarat
items.summary: Two strategic maritime projects in Gujarat have received in-principle approval from the shipping ministry. A Rs 1,570-crore ship repair facility will be built at Vadinar in the Gulf of Kutch. This facility will include a new jetty and floating dry docks for large vessels. A greenfield shipbuilding cluster is also planned for Porbandar district, aiming for significant capacity expansion.

items.title: Cash-strapped Thames Water poses big test for Burnham
items.summary: Despite returning to profit after hiking bills 40%, the deeply troubled company is far from out of the woods.

items.title: Adani Power, MSEDCL ink pact for 1,600 MW power supply
items.summary: Adani Power has signed a power supply agreement with MSEDCL. This agreement is for the supply of 1,600 MW of electricity on a long-term basis. The power will come from a new 800x2 MW ultra supercritical thermal project. This project will be established under a design, build, finance, own, and operate model. Coal linkage for the upcoming power plant has been allocated under the SHAKTI Policy.

items.title: U.S. launches fresh wave of strikes on Iran, as analysts warn conflict risks becoming 'forever war'
items.summary: Fighting in the Middle East has intensified in recent days, but could escalate further if Iran fails to cooperate, President Donald Trump said.

items.title: Emergent raises $130 mn from Creagis, Sentinel Global, Ranjan Pai's Claypond Capital
items.summary: The back-to-back rounds for Emergent come at a time when vibe coding investment has cooled in India

items.title: Cabinet approves 2 railway multitracking projects covering 4 districts in Odisha, Jharkhand
items.summary: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs approved two railway projects costing approximately Rs 3,907 crore. These projects will enhance line capacity and improve operational efficiency for Indian Railways. The proposals focus on multi-modal connectivity and logistic efficiency through integrated planning. Increased capacity will facilitate movement of people, goods, and services across four districts. These projects are essential for transporting commodities and will boost freight traffic significantly.

items.title: HDFC Bank gets RBI nod to appoint Rajiv Kumar as part-time chairman
items.summary: HDFC Bank received RBI approval for Rajiv Kumar's appointment as part-time chairman. His three-year term as chairman will begin on July 15, 2026. This appointment follows the bank's application and an earlier intimation to the regulator. The bank thanked Keki Mistry for his service as interim chairman. Mistry will continue his role as a non-executive director on the board.

items.title: Alibaba’s U.S.-listed shares rise 4% after Qwen AI set to be integrated in Apple Intelligence
items.summary: The Cyberspace Administration of China on Wednesday included Apple's AI services on a list of approved providers.

items.title: Nifty to hit 27,000 in one year? PL Capital says Middle East crisis, El Nino can play a spoil sport; names 15 top picks
items.summary: PL Capital has raised its one-year Nifty 50 target to 27,019, implying over 12% upside, while warning that the escalating West Asia conflict and a potential super El Niño could fuel inflation and hurt growth. The brokerage remains stock-specific and has identified 15 preferred large- and mid-cap picks.

items.title: Car sales jump 24% in June as auto demand stays strong
items.summary: India's passenger vehicle sales rose 24% year-on-year to 388,144 units in June, marking the third consecutive month of strong double-digit growth. Two-wheelers and three-wheelers also posted robust gains, helping the auto industry achieve record first-quarter sales despite geopolitical uncertainties, supported by steady demand, lower financing costs and new model launches.

items.title: Market wrap: Eternal, UltraTech Cement among top gainers and losers on Nifty and Sensex today
items.summary: Indian equities ended marginally higher after surrendering most intraday gains amid volatile trading. Geopolitical tensions, elevated crude prices and rupee weakness kept sentiment cautious, while analysts expect Q1 earnings and key US economic cues to drive near-term market direction.

items.title: HDFC Life Insurance Company Q1 results: Profit rises 12% YoY to ₹611 crore; VNB up 9%
items.summary: HDFC Life Insurance Company reported a 12% YoY rise in its standalone profit to ₹611.42 crore for Q1FY27. Value of new business (VNB) for the quarter rose by 9% YoY to ₹879 crore.

items.summary: Several consecutive days of strikes exchanged between the U.S. and Iran have cast uncertainty on the European Central Bank's interest rate decision next week.

items.title: Sun Pharma among 5 stocks that hit 52-week highs & rallied up to 20% in a month
items.summary: The Sensex gained 130 points on Wednesday, while five BSE 200 stocks—Paytm, Divi's Laboratories, Torrent Pharmaceuticals, Sun Pharmaceutical Industries and Apollo Hospitals—hit fresh 52-week highs, reflecting strong buying interest, improving sentiment and sustained bullish momentum.

items.title: India-UK FTA: Kolkata flags off first jewellery export consignment to UK under CETA
items.summary: Kolkata dispatched its first jewellery export to the United Kingdom under the India-UK CETA. This marks the city's entry into the first nationwide shipment following the pact's implementation. Indian gem and jewellery exporters now have zero-duty access to the UK market. This agreement is expected to significantly boost India's jewellery exports to the UK. Kolkata is poised to benefit from this transformative trade opportunity.

items.title: Quote of the day by Jim Simons: "We have three criteria. If it’s publicly traded, liquid and amenable to modeling, we trade it"
items.summary: Jim Simons' investment philosophy centered on three key criteria for trading. He prioritized assets that were publicly traded, liquid, and amenable to modeling. This approach emphasized data-driven decisions over speculation and emotions. His quantitative methods identified statistical patterns and refined models with new information. This disciplined strategy demonstrated the power of data in long-term investment success.

items.title: ASML rises 3% after hiking sales forecast for second time this year on strong AI chip demand
items.summary: ASML on Wednesday raised its guidance for a second time this year as its customers continue to ramp up their production capacity of AI chips.

items.title: Midnight social media curfew proposed for UK teens aged 16 and 17
items.summary: The government says it will improve teenagers' lives but campaigners call it a "missed opportunity".

items.title: Binance marks nine years with $156 trillion in trading volume and 323 million users
items.summary: From crypto exchange to a multi-asset financial platform, Binance has completed 9 years in operation. The years of growth, driven by its global community of more than 323 million users. $156 trillion in trade volume and counting. Read on about the journey!

items.title: Don't panic - five ways to stop your kids' endless scrolling
items.summary: Parenting experts share their tips on how to keep children's screen time under control.

items.title: Expert view: Nifty may touch 27,000 by the year end or early next year, says Rohit Srivastava of Indiacharts
items.summary: Market strategist Rohit Srivastava predicts the Nifty 50 index could reach 27,000 by the end of this year or early next year. He advises investors to buy dips and focus on growth stocks despite geopolitical uncertainties that threaten short-term stability.

items.title: 99% of exports to become duty-free with India-UK trade treaty: Amit Shah
items.summary: The India-UK free trade agreement begins today, offering zero-duty access for ninety-nine percent of Indian exports. This pact will unlock untapped potential across many labour-intensive industries and sectors. Indian professionals will also receive significant exemptions while working in the United Kingdom. Trade between the two nations is expected to double by the year 2030. This agreement is seen as a key step towards a developed India.

items.title: Tata Motors PV flags concern over proposed direct credit buys from BEE in CAFE II shifts
items.summary: Tata Motors has voiced concerns regarding a government proposal on CAFE II compliance credits. The company believes allowing direct credit purchases from the Bureau of Energy Efficiency could weaken the framework. Tata Motors insists the BEE should not originate or sell credits in its own capacity. This provision could offer a cheaper compliance option than statutory penalties. The manufacturer argues this undermines the technology-driving purpose of the CAFE regime.

items.title: India approves Rs 62,500 crore Mobile Phone Manufacturing Scheme (MPMS) to boost exports and jobs
items.summary: The union cabinet approved a new mobile phone manufacturing scheme. This initiative has an outlay of sixty-two thousand five hundred crore rupees. The scheme aims to boost domestic production and exports significantly. It also targets generating substantial direct employment opportunities. This plan will promote technological sovereignty and Indian brand development.

items.title: HDB Financial Q1 Results: Profit jumps 38% YoY to Rs 785 crore; NII up 20%
items.summary: HDB Financial Q1 Results: HDB Financial Services reported a 38% profit increase for the June quarter. Net interest income grew twenty percent, supporting overall financial performance. Assets under management reached one point two two lakh crore rupees. Asset quality showed improvement with lower Stage three loan percentages. The company experienced strong growth across its core operating metrics.

items.title: India’s unemployment rate steady at 5.5% in June
